nodes.title: Hurricane Maria Response - Week 3 Situation Report

body.processed:
Three weeks after Hurricane Maria's devastating landfall, Global Relief Partners continues to provide critical support to affected communities across the Gulf Coast.

Key Updates

  • 15 shelters remain operational, housing 3,200 residents (down from 8,400 at peak)
  • Over 350,000 meals served to date
  • Mobile medical units have treated 5,800 patients since the response began
  • Debris removal operations underway in 8 counties
  • 1,200 families have received temporary housing assistance

Challenges

  • Power outages persist in 6 counties, affecting water supply and communications
  • Road damage limiting access to rural communities
  • Rising demand for mental health support services

Needs

We continue to need volunteers with construction and case management experience. Financial donations remain critical to sustaining long-term recovery operations.
body.summary: Three weeks after Hurricane Maria's devastating landfall, Global Relief Partners continues to provide critical support to affected communities across the Gulf Coast.Key Updates15 shelters remain opera

image.alt: Hurricane recovery efforts in the Gulf Coast

body.processed:
Ten days after the devastating earthquake in central Turkey, search and rescue operations have concluded and the focus has shifted to providing sustained humanitarian assistance to affected populations.

Current Situation

  • Approximately 180,000 people displaced from their homes
  • Over 4,000 buildings severely damaged or destroyed
  • Temperatures dropping below freezing, increasing urgency for winter shelter

Our Response

  • 10,000 winterized shelter kits distributed to families
  • Field hospitals treating an average of 200 patients per day
  • 25 water purification systems operational, producing 500,000 liters daily
  • Psychosocial support teams providing counseling in 12 locations

Next Steps

We are scaling up transitional shelter construction and planning for medium-term recovery programming, including livelihood support and school rehabilitation.
body.summary: Ten days after the devastating earthquake in central Turkey, search and rescue operations have concluded and the focus has shifted to providing sustained humanitarian assistance to affected population

body.processed:
Global Relief Partners has completed initial damage assessments in 12 counties across Iowa, Illinois, and Missouri following catastrophic spring flooding.

Assessment Findings

  • Estimated 6,500 homes with significant flood damage
  • 28,000 people displaced or directly affected
  • Agricultural losses affecting 200,000 acres of farmland
  • 18 road bridges damaged or impassable

Immediate Actions

  • Opened 8 emergency shelters in coordination with local emergency management
  • Deploying mobile kitchens to serve 5,000 meals daily
  • Distributing flood cleanup kits with mold prevention supplies
  • Activating volunteer teams for mud-out and debris removal operations
